Notice Of Application And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ALAN MARKS, ELLEN MARKS, MARKS FAMILY TRUST'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Motions to bring all parties to a dispute before the Court are liberally granted, largely for efficiency reasons. Even with new parties, the case could easily be tried within two years of filing. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in with the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael Drews of Campbell Killin Brittan & Ray LLC (303-394-7209) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party,

Attorney, Adrianne C. Duncan of Clapp Moroney Vucinich et al (925-734-0990) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ALAN MARKS, ELLEN MARKS and MARKS FAMILY TRUST'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts showing that Mr. Marks knew that the deck, stairways, landings and guardrails were in a dangerous condition and chose not to repair them to make them safe for persons who visited premises. This is sufficient to allege entitlement to punitive damages based on malice for despicable conduct carried on with willful and conscious disregard for the safety of others. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Amended Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Bifurcate Trial Against Paragon Custom Homes, Inc.
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Bifurcate Trial Against Paragon Custom Homes, Inc.
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Drews of Campbell Killin Brittan & Ray, LLC telephone number 303-394-7209 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Sandon Olson Jr.
Adrianne Duncan of Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman & Scheley, PC telephone number 925-734-0990 for Defendants Alan Marks, Ellen Marks and Marks Family Trust.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Sandor Olson's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted on two conditions and its motion to bifurcate trial against Paragon Custom Homes is off calendar. Leave to add a prayer for punitive damages against Alan Marks and to substitute Paragon Custom Homes, Inc. as Doe 1 is granted on the following two conditions: 1) plaintiff must serve full non-evasive responses to the following deemed- served interrogatories and document requests within ten days: state all facts that support your claims against Paragon and your request for punitive damages against Mr. Marks, identify all persons with knowledge of those facts and which facts they have knowledge about, and identify and produce all documents referring to such facts; and 2) plaintiffs must agree that Paragon and Mr. Marks may file a motion for summary judgment or adjudication on the claims against Paragon and the request for punitive damages against Mr. Marks on 30 calendar days' notice with opposition papers due 14 calendar days before the hearing and reply papers due 5 calendar days before the hearing. With these conditions, defendants suffer no prejudice from the granting of the motion for leave to file a first amended complaint. The motion to amend is favored by the strong California policies of allowing amendments to pleadings and deciding cases on their merits. Courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial. (Mesler v. Bragg Management Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296-297.) Defendants' argument that there is no basis for punitive damages against Mr. Marks is best addressed on a motion to strike or summary motion, not a motion to amend. The motion to bifurcate was withdrawn in the reply memorandum. Order signed in open Court. A hearing on the motion for summary judgment can occur August 22, 2016 or earlier.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
